commit.message:
fix(sync): warn when plugin has no effective clients and is skipped (#240)

* feat!: inline plugin skills config, workspace schema v2 (#233)

BREAKING CHANGE: skill selection is now configured inline on each
plugin entry instead of top-level disabledSkills/enabledSkills arrays.

- Add PluginSkillsConfig type: string[] (allowlist) or {exclude:string[]} (blocklist)
- Add skills field to PluginEntrySchema object variant
- Add version field to WorkspaceConfigSchema
- Update getAllSkillsFromPlugins and collectPluginSkills to read inline skills
- Add pluginSkillsMode to SkillInfo for caller convenience
- Update workspace-modify and user-workspace write functions to write inline
- Simplify plugin-skills CLI: remove inAllowlistMode detection, use SkillInfo.pluginSkillsMode
- Add migrateWorkspaceSkillsV1toV2 / migrateUserWorkspaceSkillsV1toV2 called on sync
- Bump to v1.0.0, add CHANGELOG.md

Migration: automatic on next workspace sync. No manual action required.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(sync): warn when plugin has no effective clients and is skipped

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Move Azure.Provisioning.Storage from sdk/provisioning to sdk/storage (#57084)

* Move Azure.Provisioning.Storage from sdk/provisioning to sdk/storage

Move Azure.Provisioning.Storage to its corresponding service directory
(sdk/storage/) as part of the plan to colocate provisioning RP libraries
with their respective service directories.

- Moved sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning.Storage/ to sdk/storage/Azure.Provisioning.Storage/ via git mv
- Added Directory.Build.props at new location with provisioning-specific build settings
- Duplicated shared test helpers (ProvisioningTestBase, ProvisioningTestEnvironment, Trycep) into the test project
- Fixed relative project references in the test .csproj
- Added Azure.Provisioning.Storage artifact to sdk/storage/ci.mgmt.yml
- Updated sdk/storage/service.projects with mgmt SDKType condition for provisioning projects
- Removed Storage entries from sdk/provisioning/ci.data.yml, service.projects, and Azure.Provisioning.sln

Build: 0 warnings, 0 errors
Tests: 12 passed (3 tests x 4 target frameworks)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Consolidate provisioning test helpers into shared location

Move ProvisioningTestBase, ProvisioningTestEnvironment, and Trycep from
duplicated copies in each test project into
sdk/provisioning/common/ProvisioningTestShared/ and include them via
Compile Include in the test .csproj files.

- Created sdk/provisioning/common/ProvisioningTestShared/ with the 3 shared files
- Removed duplicated copies from keyvault and storage test projects
- Updated Azure.Provisioning.Tests.csproj, Azure.Provisioning.KeyVault.Tests.csproj,
  and Azure.Provisioning.Storage.Tests.csproj to reference shared sources
- Fixed Azure.Provisioning.Tests.csproj Storage project reference path

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move shared provisioning test helpers to common/ProvisioningTestShared

Move from sdk/provisioning/common/ProvisioningTestShared/ to
common/ProvisioningTestShared/ to follow the repo convention
(alongside common/ManagementTestShared/).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Directory.Build.targets to reference shared test helpers from new location

Update sdk/provisioning/Directory.Build.targets to include shared test
helpers from common/ProvisioningTestShared/ instead of the old path at
sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/tests/. This ensures all unmoved
provisioning test projects continue to build correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(security): eliminate script injection vulnerabilities in action.yml (#85)

* Initial plan

* fix(security): prevent script injection in action.yml

- Pass `inputs.atmos-config-path` through env var and quote it in
  `realpath` call to prevent command injection
- Pass `inputs.base-ref` and `inputs.skip-checkout` through env vars
  and use a bash array in the `git checkout` call to prevent injection
- Remove the `affected-args` intermediate step that built a raw shell
  string from unquoted `${{ inputs.* }}` expressions
- Replace `eval` in both `atmos describe affected` invocations with
  bash arrays built from env vars, eliminating arbitrary code execution
  via user-controlled inputs (`atmos-stack`, `identity`, etc.)

Co-authored-by: nitrocode <7775707+nitrocode@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(security): DRY up repeated args-building logic between affected steps

Extract shared argument construction into a dedicated 'Build atmos
affected command args' step that writes args (one per line) to
${RUNNER_TEMP}/atmos-affected-args. Both 'affected-pro' and 'affected'
steps then read them back with mapfile, passing only the step-specific
flag (--upload or --file=) inline. No eval, no injection surface.

Co-authored-by: nitrocode <7775707+nitrocode@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: nitrocode <7775707+nitrocode@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Andriy Knysh <aknysh@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix unstuck PR loop to advance draft PRs instead of incorrectly skipping them (#609)

* Initial plan

* Fix unstuck PR loop to use check_pr_merge_status like advance_issue_pr

The main PR loop in run_unstuck only called has_failing_checks_for_pr(),
which returns false for draft PRs with no checks, incorrectly logging
"checks OK, skipping". Now uses check_pr_merge_status() to properly
handle draft PRs (marking them ready for review), agent WIP, not-yet-
mergeable PRs (checking failures, approving workflows, enabling auto-
merge), and mergeable PRs — matching the advancement logic already used
in advance_issue_pr.

Co-authored-by: randymarsh77 <1606704+randymarsh77@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: randymarsh77 <1606704+randymarsh77@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: batch event creation from availability heatmap (#126)

* feat: batch event creation from availability heatmap

Extend the availability results page so admins can select multiple dates
from the heatmap and create events in batch with a single consolidated
notification email instead of one email per event.

- Extended createEventsFromAvailability() with per-date locations and
  description passthrough
- New sendBatchEventsFromAvailabilityNotification() email function that
  sends one consolidated email per member with all events listed,
  segmented by availability status (available/maybe/no-response)
- New sendBatchEventsCreatedWebhook() for a single Discord notification
  listing all created events

- Results heatmap now supports batch selection mode (admin-only):
  checkboxes, Select Top 5, Select All, floating action bar
- New batch route at /groups/:groupId/availability/:requestId/batch
  with 2-step configure→review flow
- Per-date location fields with 'Apply to All' helper
- Success banner on availability page after batch creation

- 40 new tests across 4 test files covering:
  - Batch creation service (per-date locations, description, fallbacks)
  - Batch email notifications (segmentation, preferences, edge cases)
  - Batch webhook (embed formatting, singular/plural)
  - Batch route action (auth, CSRF, validation, IDOR prevention)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add batch operations skill and update notifications skill

- New greenroom-batch-operations skill documenting multi-select UI,
  batch creation, consolidated notifications, and multi-step form patterns
- Updated greenroom-notifications skill with batch notification details
  including segmentation logic and test file references

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: 再接続時に PTY 出力をリプレイする機能を追加

セッションごとに最大 100KB の出力リングバッファを保持し、
WebSocket 再接続時にバッファ内容を replay メッセージとして送信する。

- SessionManager に outputBuffer, appendOutput(), getOutputBuffer() を追加
- PTY onData で出力をバッファに蓄積
- 新規 WS 接続時に type:'replay' メッセージを送信
- クライアント側で replay 受信時に term.reset() → write で復元
- バッファ関連のユニットテスト 4 件追加

commit.message:
fix(supervisor): send bridge heartbeat before terminal for fast workers

Workers that complete without emitting heartbeat events left the lease
in STARTING state. The terminal heartbeat then failed because
STARTING → COMPLETING is not a valid transition (only RUNNING/HEARTBEATING
→ COMPLETING is allowed).

Now the supervisor sends a non-terminal bridge heartbeat before the
terminal one. This advances the lease STARTING → RUNNING, then the
terminal heartbeat completes RUNNING → COMPLETING. When the lease is
already past STARTING (from regular stream heartbeats), the bridge
heartbeat is handled as a normal heartbeat progression.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: The open source AI engineering platform. MLflow enables teams of all sizes to debug, evaluate, monitor, and optimize production-quality AI agents, LLM applications, and ML models while controlling costs and managing access to models and data.

commit.message:
fix(supervisor): transition lease LEASED → STARTING before heartbeat loop

The worker supervisor now transitions the lease from LEASED to STARTING
after spawning the worker process, before the heartbeat streaming loop
begins. Previously the lease stayed in LEASED state and heartbeats were
rejected because the heartbeat service only accepts STARTING, RUNNING,
or HEARTBEATING states.

Adds LeaseTransitionerPort to WorkerSupervisorDependencies (optional,
for backward compatibility) and wires the TransitionService adapter
in the automation runtime.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: reorder LOAN form - pagador/tomador right after fecha

Move "¿Quién pagó?" and "¿Quién recibió?" fields to appear right
after the date field in Préstamo mode, since the category changes
depending on who lent/received. New field order for LOAN:

1. Dirección (Prestaron/Devolvieron)
2. Fecha
3. ¿Quién pagó? / ¿Quién recibió?
4. Categoría
5. Nota
6. Monto
7. Cuenta receptora
8. Método de pago

Applied to both form templates (loading and normal render).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: conti
repository.description: Personal and family finance app

commit.message:
fix(worker-runtime): handle spawn ENOENT error gracefully

The CopilotCliAdapter crashed the entire Node.js process when the
'gh' binary was not found because the child_process 'error' event
had no listener. Now:

- Added onError to CliProcess interface
- createDefaultProcessSpawner() wires child.on('error')
- startRun() captures spawn errors as stderr, transitions to
  completed with exit code -1, and resolves the exit promise
  so downstream steps (stream/collect/finalize) don't hang

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(import): add rate-limit retry with backoff to AI task classifier

The AI classifier now retries 429/503 errors up to 3 times, honouring
the Retry-After header and 'Please wait N seconds' body hints, with
exponential backoff as fallback. A 500ms inter-batch delay proactively
spreads token usage to avoid hitting the GitHub Models API rate limit.

Adds RetryOptions config, injectable sleep/onRetry on AiClassifierDeps,
and CLI progress feedback during retries.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: require 2+ participants for green button on SPLIT movements

The submit button now only turns green for shared expenses (gastos
compartidos) when there are at least 2 participants, not just 1.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(import): preserve full markdown as description and improve LLM extraction

- Store entire raw markdown as description instead of only extracting
  ## Description and ## Goal sections (all content now preserved)
- Inject raw file content as description for LLM-extracted tasks
- Remove description from LLM extraction fields (saves tokens, avoids summarization)
- Add comprehensive status mapping (not-started → BACKLOG, done → DONE, etc.)
- Improve dependency pattern examples (M15-003, GH#123, JIRA-42)
- Improve suggestedFileScope guidance (search any section, not just Context Files)
- Add 4 new tests for full markdown preservation and dependency extraction

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: build complete TaskPacket to prevent worker dispatch crashes

buildTaskPacket() only produced 3 of 19 required TaskPacket fields,
causing 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading map)' when
generatePrompt() accessed stop_conditions, role, and other missing
fields. This was the same class of bug as the prior title crash
(194c20d) — an incomplete packet assembly.

Changes:
- Expand buildTaskPacket() to produce all 19 TaskPacket fields
  (role, stop_conditions, repository, workspace, context, policies,
  expected_output, etc.)
- Add mapTaskTypeToRole() helper and DEFAULT_STOP_CONDITIONS
- Change SupervisorRunContext.taskPacket type from Record<string,
  unknown> to TaskPacket for compile-time safety
- Update test assertions to verify the full packet shape

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: モバイルでタップ時キーボードがすぐ隠れる問題を修正

- キーボードトランジション中の visualViewport resize を抑制
  (_keyboardTransition フラグで 400ms 間 resize チェーンをブロック)
- visualViewport resize ハンドラを 150ms debounce 化
- タッチデバイスでは pointerdown での focus を抑制し touchend に統一
  (double-focus による thrash を防止)
- 接続時の不要な setTimeout 再 focus を削除
- focusTerminal() ヘルパーでキーボード表示フローを一元管理

commit.message:
Advance agent-created PRs in unstuck workflow (#608)

* Initial plan

* Add PR advancement to unstuck workflow when issue already exists

When the unstuck workflow finds an existing issue tracking a failing main
branch build, it now checks whether the agent has created a linked PR and
advances it through the merge pipeline:
- Converts draft PRs to ready for review
- Approves pending workflow runs
- Enables auto-merge

commit.message:
feat: add configurable hostname and admin user password (#187)

* feat(bootstrap): add configurable hostname and admin user password

- Add `hostname` and `admin_user_password` to config.toml template
- Fix /etc/hosts to properly map 127.0.0.1→localhost and 127.0.1.1→hostname
- Set admin user password via password_hash('sha512') for Cockpit login
- Require `hostname` in bootstrap playbook config validation

* docs: update documentation for hostname and admin_user_password config

Update bootstrap, config, and getting-started docs to reflect new
config.toml settings. Clarify admin_user_password is optional (for
Cockpit login) while hostname is required.

* fix(fail2ban): use systemd backend for SSH jails (#189)

* fix(fail2ban): use systemd backend for SSH jails instead of missing auth.log

* docs(fail2ban): document jail configuration and systemd backend usage

* docs update

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ansible_user): decouple admin user creation from password setting

Admin user creation was gated on admin_user_password being set, which
skipped user creation entirely when password was empty. Split into two
tasks: always create user, conditionally set password. Also use
update_password: on_create for idempotent reruns.

* fix(bootstrap): tighten /etc/hosts regex to avoid prefix matching

^127\.0\.0\.1 also matches 127.0.0.10. Add \s after the IP to ensure
only the exact address followed by whitespace is matched.

* test(ansible_runner): assert hostname in bootstrap required keys

* docs: clarify admin_user_password is optional across all docs

Mark admin_user_password as optional (only needed for Cockpit login)
in first-deployment, bootstrap, secrets, and cockpit documentation.

* docs: clarify optional password and fallback in layers and cockpit docs

* fix(bootstrap): add hostname validation before VPS connection

* fix(ansible_user): add no_log to password task to prevent secret leakage

* chore: sync config.example.toml with TEMPLATE in user_config.rs

Adds missing keys: hostname, admin_user_password, bichon_*,
headscale_subdomain, paperless_subdomain, paperless_tailscale_ip,
restic_*, tailscale_login_server, and vdirsyncer_*.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

making `auberge ansible run --tags fail2ban` unresolvable.

commit.message:
Add Arduino Leonardo force-feedback wheel firmware

Complete firmware implementation for a DIY force-feedback steering wheel
using Arduino Leonardo, BTS7960 motor driver, and quadrature encoder.

Includes: encoder reader, motor driver, settings management, EEPROM
persistence, serial protocol handler, steering math, and main sketch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: restructure task packet to match generatePrompt expected format

Worker dispatch failed with 'Cannot read properties of undefined
(reading title)' because buildTaskPacket returned a flat camelCase
object but generatePrompt expected a nested snake_case structure:
{ task_id, task: { title, task_type, acceptance_criteria, ... } }

Restructured buildTaskPacket to produce the expected nested format
and updated all test assertions accordingly.

commit.message:
feat: worker prompt management UI + init seeding + runtime wiring

Add complete prompt template management across the stack:

Backend:
- Add PromptTemplatesModule with CRUD API (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /prompt-templates)
- Seed 6 default prompt templates in factory init (one per agent role)
- Link prompt templates to agent profiles via prompt_template_id
- Wire prompt templates into worker dispatch — resolve custom prompt
  from agent profile at dispatch time, pass through RunContext
- generatePrompt() uses custom template when set, falls back to
  hardcoded ROLE_PROMPTS

UI:
- Add /prompts list page with table, role filter, and create dialog
- Add /prompts/:id detail/editor page with large textarea for prompt text
- Add Prompts nav item to sidebar
- Replace raw UUID field in CreateProfileDialog with template picker
  dropdown filtered by pool role
- Add TanStack Query hooks for prompt template CRUD

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix off-by-one in experiment count summary

The loop variable $experiment is incremented past the last iteration,
causing the summary to report N+1 experiments. Introduce an explicit
$experimentsRun counter computed inside the loop.

commit.message:
sessions: `Generate New Action...` (#301604)

* feat: add prompt for adding a run action to the current session

* feat: enhance run script action with Copilot assistance option

* feat: update Copilot prompt handling in run script action

* feat: update accessibility options and placeholder types in monaco editor

* feat: rename Copilot action label and description for clarity

* feat: add generate new action option via Copilot in run script action

* Fix 'Generate New Action...' on new session page

On the new session page, chatService.sendRequest() cannot be used because
the chat session hasn't been initialized yet. Detect isUntitled sessions and
route through NewChatViewPane.sendQuery() instead, which sets the editor
value and triggers the normal _send() flow (including slash command expansion).

Also adds prefillInput/sendQuery public methods to NewChatWidget and
NewChatViewPane, and injects IViewsService into RunScriptContribution.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename slash command to /generate-run-commands and improve prompt

- Rename add-run-action.prompt.md → generate-run-commands.prompt.md
- Update runScriptAction.ts to send /generate-run-commands
- Rewrite prompt with:
  - Two task schema fields: inSessions (required) and
    runOptions.runOn: worktreeCreated (optional, for setup commands)
  - Smart inference: reads project files to determine commands;
    only asks the user if ambiguous
  - Modify mode: if run commands already exist, treat as an update
  - Merge-safe: always preserves existing tasks.json entries

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* tidy

* Clear editor after successful send on new session page

After sendRequestForNewSession completes successfully, clear the editor
value so it doesn't persist when the user navigates back to a new session.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix prompt not activating: use chatWidget.acceptInput() for active sessions

chatService.sendRequest() bypasses the chat widget's prompt file resolution
logic (_applyPromptFileIfSet). Use IChatWidgetService.getWidgetBySessionResource()
and call acceptInput('/generate-run-commands') instead, which goes through
the full input pipeline including slash command parsing and prompt file
attachment.

Also removes now-unused IChatService and ChatAgentLocation imports.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add new prompt for adding run actions

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* tidy

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
sessions: built-in skills (#301607)

* sessions: add built-in skills infrastructure

Add a 'built-in skills' concept mirroring the existing 'built-in prompts'
pattern, so that skills bundled with the Sessions app are always available.

- Create src/vs/sessions/skills/ directory for bundled SKILL.md files
- Bundle vs/sessions/skills/**/SKILL.md in build config
- AgenticPromptsService: discover and parse built-in skills from the
  bundled directory, override findAgentSkills() to merge them at lowest
  priority (user/workspace skills override by name), extend listPromptFiles
  and listPromptFilesForStorage for skill type
- Tree view: handle BUILTIN_STORAGE group for skills category

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* sessions: add built-in 'update-skills' skill

First built-in skill that guides the agent to capture major repository
learnings by creating or updating skills and instructions. Triggered
when the user says 'learn!' or when significant reusable knowledge is
discovered during a session.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* sessions: support edit-and-save-to-override for built-in skills

Extend the built-in prompt save-to-override flow to also work for
built-in skills. When editing a built-in skill, users can save it to
workspace or user location, which overrides the built-in version.

- Gate the in-memory editing session on both prompt and skill types
- Resolve save target directories using the actual prompt type
- Preserve skill directory structure ({name}/SKILL.md) when saving
- Generalize UI labels from 'prompt override' to 'override'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* sessions: address PR review feedback for built-in skills

- Sanitize built-in skill name/description (strip XML tags, truncate)
  to match the same safety applied to other skill sources
- Fix JSDoc: all sources override built-ins, not just user/workspace
- Remove unnecessary 'as unknown as PromptsStorage' cast in tree view

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* sessions: refine editor options types for accessibility and font settings

commit.message:
feat(cortex): implement P1 dialectical query orchestrator

- Add cortex/Ranking.ts: rankPages/rankBooks/rankVolumes/rankShelves
  use cosine similarity against entity representative vectors;
  spillToWarm('page') scores the full corpus as fallback

- Rewrite cortex/Query.ts (P1-E1): full dialectical orchestrator
  - HOT path: rankPages() over resident hotpath entries
  - Warm spill: spillToWarm('page') when hot path is insufficient
  - buildMetroid() with book medoid + page candidates
  - detectKnowledgeGap() from metroid result
  - getInducedNeighborSubgraph() + solveOpenTSP() for coherence path
  - queryHitCount increment + runPromotionSweep()
  - QueryResult now fully populated (coherencePath, metroid, knowledgeGap)

- Add tests/cortex/Ranking.test.ts: 12 tests covering empty inputs,
  cosine scoring, descending order, topK, spillToWarm all tiers

- Update tests/cortex/Query.test.ts: add assertions for new fields
  (coherencePath, metroid, knowledgeGap); preserve all existing
  behavioral checks; update describe label to reflect new orchestrator

All 274 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: cortex
repository.description: Clustered Ontic Routing Through Entangled eXchanges

commit.message:
Add Export-API step to post-release version increment flow (#57039)

* Add Export-API step to post-release version increment flow

After incrementing the package version, run Export-API.ps1 on the
service directory so that updated API listing files are included in
the version bump PR. Preview feature flags can add APIs in beta that
are absent in GA, so when the version bumps (e.g. 1.0.0 to
1.1.0-beta.1) the public API surface may change and the listing
files need to be regenerated.

Also update sparse checkout to include .cs files for the service
directory so the ExportApi build target can compile.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add shared source paths to sparse checkout for Export-API build

Include Azure.Core, Azure.Core.Amqp, and Azure.ResourceManager shared
source directories so Export-API.ps1 can compile SDKs that reference
shared sources outside their service directory.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove .cs filtering from sparse checkout per review feedback

Simplify by checking out all files under /sdk instead of maintaining
an allowlist of shared source paths. Export-API needs a full build,
so .cs files are required anyway.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Initial commit with copilot-instructions template

feat: implement OpenTSPSolver, MetroidBuilder, KnowledgeGapDetector (P1)

- cortex/OpenTSPSolver.ts: greedy nearest-neighbor open-path TSP heuristic
  with deterministic start (lex smallest node) and tiebreaker
- cortex/MetroidBuilder.ts: dialectical probe builder selecting m1 (thesis)
  and m2 (antithesis medoid) with Matryoshka dimensional unwinding across
  standard tiers [32,64,128,256,512,768,1024,2048]; computes frozen synthesis
  centroid c
- cortex/KnowledgeGapDetector.ts: detectKnowledgeGap + buildCuriosityProbe
  with deterministic SHA-256 probeId
- cortex/QueryResult.ts: add coherencePath, metroid, knowledgeGap fields
- cortex/Query.ts: populate new QueryResult fields with flat-query defaults
- 32 new tests across OpenTSPSolver, MetroidBuilder, KnowledgeGapDetector

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: support any-prefix dependency refs and external refs (M12-006, M8, etc.)

extractDependencyRefs() only matched T-prefixed refs (T001, T002),
silently dropping M-prefixed refs like M12-006 and M8 used in many
real-world backlogs.

- Generalized regex to match any alphanumeric prefix: M12-006, M8,
  T001, GH#123, JIRA-42, etc.
- Updated extractExternalRef() to handle non-T filename prefixes
  (M12-005-llm-provider.md → M12-005)
- Both markdown link and plain-text formats supported
- Parenthetical annotations stripped naturally by word boundary matching

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve npm audit vulnerabilities and Aspire Docker test cleanup

- Bump minimatch 9.0.6→9.0.9 (ReDoS: GHSA-7r86-cg39-jmmj, GHSA-23c5-xmqv-rm74)
- Bump rollup 4.58.0→4.59.0 (path traversal: GHSA-mw96-cpmx-2vgc)
- Add --name to squad aspire Docker command to prevent unnamed orphan containers
- Align test and CLI on shared container name (squad-aspire-dashboard)
- Add SIGINT/SIGTERM/exit handlers to aspire integration test for cleanup

commit.message:
fix: resolve artifact paths to absolute and create directories before dispatch

Workers failed with ENOENT on artifacts/<taskId>/packets/input because:
1. Paths were relative with no base directory
2. Parent directories were never created

Fix:
- CLI startup now sets WORKSPACES_ROOT and ARTIFACTS_ROOT env vars
  pointing to ~/.copilot-factory/workspaces and artifacts
- resolveSpawnContext builds absolute paths using resolved artifactsRoot
- mkdirSync(packetInputPath, { recursive: true }) ensures dirs exist

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(hippocampus): implement HierarchyBuilder, FastNeighborInsert, and upgrade Ingest

- HierarchyBuilder: cluster pages→books→volumes→shelves using cosine-distance
  medoid selection; persist each tier; run promotion sweeps after each level
- FastNeighborInsert: build sparse SemanticNeighbor graph (NOT Hebbian);
  forward + symmetric reverse edges; degree-bounded with eviction; marks
  affected volumes dirty; runs promotion sweep for new pages
- Ingest: replace manual single-book construction with buildHierarchy;
  add insertSemanticNeighbors call; extend IngestResult with volumes/shelves
- Tests: 11 HierarchyBuilder tests + 8 FastNeighborInsert tests (230 total pass)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: store local_checkout_path in repository for worker workspace resolution

Workers failed with 'git symbolic-ref --short HEAD ENOENT' because
resolveSpawnContext passed repository.remoteUrl (a URL) where the
workspace manager expected a local filesystem path.

- Add local_checkout_path column to repository table (migration 0006)
- Store cwd as local_checkout_path during factory init
- Store request.path as local_checkout_path during API import
- Use localCheckoutPath in resolveSpawnContext (fallback to remoteUrl)
- Update inline CREATE TABLE in 3 test files + column count assertion

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: revise all READMEs and add consolidated PDF user guide

- Rewrite root README.md: updated directory tree, pipeline descriptions,
  removed all ep1_ibc_ibt_analysis references, highlighted
  ep_structure_analysis as current scientific focus, corrected
  data source URLs and setup instructions
- Rewrite scripts/README.md: accurate subdirectory descriptions,
  ep_structure_analysis scientific summary, corrected main/ description
  as final publication figure scripts (not 'main analyses')
- Update scripts/main/README.md: remove ep1_ibc_ibt_analysis dependency
  references from S2/S3 figures
- Update scripts/ck_subterms_analysis/README.md: fix prerequisite to
  reference cluster results (not ep1_ibc_ibt_analysis), correct EP1
  count to 444 cyclones
- Rewrite scripts/preprocess_data/README.md: accurate script list,
  correct output paths, proper run order
- Create scripts/exploratory/README.md: new README listing all 15
  exploratory scripts with purpose, inputs, and outputs
- Create scripts/documentation/compile_docs.py: reproducible script
  that collects 9 READMEs, generates combined Markdown, and compiles
  to docs/user_guide_repository_readmes.pdf via pandoc
- Add docs/user_guide_combined.md and docs/user_guide_repository_readmes.pdf

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(import): replace MD parsing with LLM extraction via Copilot SDK

Add LLM-based task extraction to the import pipeline using
@github/copilot-sdk. The new extractor sends markdown file content
to a Copilot session with a schema-aware system prompt and parses
the structured JSON response into validated ImportedTask objects.

- Add llm-task-extractor.ts with extractTasksWithLlm(), parseLlmResponse(),
  and createCopilotClientFactory() using dynamic imports
- Define CopilotClientLike/CopilotSessionLike interfaces for testability
- Update discoverMarkdownTasks() to try LLM first, fall back per-file
  to deterministic regex parsing when SDK is unavailable
- Skip AI classifier for LLM-extracted tasks (already classified)
- Wire lazy SDK initialization into ImportService
- Add 19 tests covering extraction, failures, and cleanup

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement 13 critical gaps: backtester, odds engine, correlation modeling, rotation tracker, validators, logger, game log cache, rate limiter, st.cache_data, and system health dashboard

- engine/backtester.py: historical backtest engine (Monte Carlo vs game logs)
- engine/odds_engine.py: American odds ↔ probability, true edge, EV, vig
- engine/rotation_tracker.py: minutes trend/role-change tracking, trend factor
- engine/entry_optimizer.py: get_correlation_coefficient + calculate_parlay_probability_with_correlation
- engine/bankroll.py: odds_to_payout_multiplier (delegates to odds_engine)
- engine/minutes_model.py: add game_logs param to project_player_minutes
- data/validators.py: CSV structure, freshness, integrity, prop validation
- data/data_manager.py: st.cache_data decorators, clear_all_caches(), get_data_health_report()
- data/platform_fetcher.py: odds helper functions after _now_str()
- data/game_log_cache.py: persistent JSON cache for player game logs
- utils/logger.py: rotating-file + console structured logger
- utils/rate_limiter.py: token-bucket rate limiter with circuit breaker
- pages/12_📊_Backtester.py: Streamlit backtester UI
- app.py: System Health expander with data freshness metrics

commit.message:
feat: automate Cloudflare DNS record creation in Ansible roles (#181)

* Initial plan

* feat: automate Cloudflare DNS record creation in Ansible roles

Co-authored-by: sripwoud <38692952+sripwoud@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(dns_record): add input validation, error handling, and zone ID caching

- Assert required variables upfront for clear failure messages
- Add failed_when checking Cloudflare API success field to catch
  logical failures returned with HTTP 200
- Add changed_when for accurate idempotency reporting
- Use verbosity-gated no_log so errors are visible with -vvv
- Cache zone ID across multiple role inclusions in same play
- Add meta/main.yml matching project conventions

* docs: update DNS documentation for automatic provisioning

DNS A records are now auto-provisioned during app deployment via the
dns_record role. Update first-deployment guide, managing-records,
batch-operations, cloudflare-setup, infrastructure-layers, ansible-roles
guide, and troubleshooting docs to reflect this change.

* feat(cockpit): add dns_record integration with Tailscale IP

Cockpit binds to the Tailscale interface, so its DNS A record must
point to the Tailscale IP rather than the public VPS IP.

* fix(dns_record): validate Cloudflare success flag on all API calls

- Add failed_when on zone lookup and record lookup GET requests
- Check for exactly 1 zone result instead of just non-empty
- Use verbosity-gated no_log on assert task

* fix(dns_record): domain-scoped cache, IPv4 validation, drift detection

- Cache zone IDs in a dict keyed by domain (safe for multi-domain plays)
- Validate dns_record_ip matches IPv4 format in assert block
- Fail explicitly when multiple A records found for same FQDN
- Update task now triggers on IP, TTL, or proxied changes (not just IP)

* docs: add cockpit to integrated apps lists, fix troubleshooting causes

- Include cockpit in all dns_record integration app lists
- Broaden batch-operations example to include all non-integrated apps
- Rewrite troubleshooting causes to reflect realistic failure scenarios

* fix(dns_record): strict IPv4 octet-range validation, update docs example

- Tighten regex to reject out-of-range octets (e.g. 999.999.999.999)
- Update managing-records example to show only non-integrated apps

* fix(dns_record): prevent token leakage, use idiomatic regex test

- Keep no_log: true on all URI tasks to prevent Bearer token exposure
  in CI logs or shared terminals at any verbosity level
- Surface Cloudflare API errors via separate fail tasks that log only
  sanitized json.errors field
- Switch is regex() to is match() for Ansible idiom consistency

* fix(dns_record): include HTTP status and msg fallback in error messages

All fail tasks now surface status code and Ansible's msg field when
Cloudflare json.errors is unavailable (timeouts, TLS errors, non-JSON
responses).

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: factory start/pause toggle for operator UI

Add global start/pause control for the factory production line:

Backend:
- AutomationService starts paused by default; runCycle() skips scheduling
  and dispatch when paused. Active workers continue to completion.
- New FactoryStateController at /api/factory/state with GET (read state),
  POST /start (resume), POST /pause (stop scheduling)
- State changes broadcast via WebSocket on Queue channel

Frontend:
- useFactoryState/useStartFactory/usePauseFactory hooks
- Dashboard header shows running/paused badge + Start/Pause button
- WebSocket-driven cache invalidation for real-time state sync

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(init): add starter configuration setup for pools, profiles, and policies

After project/repo creation and optional task import, factory init now
prompts: 'Set up starter configuration? (pools, profiles, policies) [y/N]'

When accepted, creates:
- 1 default policy set (review, merge, validation, security, budget)
- Developer pool (max_concurrency: 3, copilot-cli runtime)
- Reviewer pool (max_concurrency: 3, matches dev throughput)
- Lead reviewer pool (max_concurrency: 2, handles bursts via queue)
- 3 agent profiles linking each pool to the default policy set

All resources are tweakable via the operator UI afterward.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
